mechanism_narrative: A falling 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supports risk appetite; larger declines in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 4.49 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle after prior tightening
failure_condition: Reject if months with declining 3-month FEDFUNDS produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of observations or have mean forward returns no higher than the full-sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals contraction in residential investment and household credit creation; when housing starts break lower, expected earnings deteriorate and S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month minimum or falls more than 15% year over year
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST vintage value was 1229.0 for 2007-10-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered mean returns exceed non-triggered mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE over a short window signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future income growth; a material increase in unemployment predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE vintage value was 4.7 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional negative-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Weak RSAFS growth combined with slowing PAYEMS identifies a demand shock in which households face both lower spending momentum and weaker job creation; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is negative and PAYEMS 3-month change is below its trailing 12-month median
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
series:PAYEMS
fact:RSAFS vintage value was 385753.0 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 138467.0 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS trigger has no lower 60-day mean S&P 500 return than either single-series trigger alone or if its hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A declining INDPRO trend signals reduced factory output and margin pressure for cyclical firms; weaker industrial production momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO vintage value was 113.9019 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month growth below 0% is followed by higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with positive INDPRO 3-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL inflation while FEDFUNDS remains elevated creates a restrictive policy mix that limits the Federal Reserve's ability to ease; this interaction raises discount-rate and recession risk, predicting negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is above 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 4%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 210.565 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 4.49 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS joint trigger does not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with high CPIAUCSL alone or high FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDPC1 growth indicates expanding aggregate demand and supports forward earnings expectations; higher quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 11659.3 for 2007-07-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth periods have 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean or if the positive-return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly RSAFS momentum signals resilient household spending and near-term revenue support for consumer-facing firms; stronger retail-sales momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS vintage value was 385753.0 for 2007-11-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS month-over-month growth above 0% is not associated with a higher 5-day S&P 500 positive-return frequency than RSAFS month-over-month growth below 0%.
